prompt_text:
You are shown a SKELETON view of a java repository: class headers,
inheritance, method signatures, and attributes — but NO method bodies.

Use this signature-level view to detect structural violations of Single Responsibility Principle (SRP):
- Fat interfaces / bloated classes (many unrelated method groups)
- Clients forced to implement methods they don't need (abstract methods / NotImplementedError / empty overrides)
- Inheritance hierarchies where subclasses alter the contract shape
- Broad AP...
raw_response:
{
  "findings": [
    {
      "file_path": "src/main/java/tools/jackson/core/json/UTF8StreamJsonParser.java",
      "entity_name": "UTF8StreamJsonParser",
      "entity_type": "class",
      "line_start": 20,
      "line_end": 4341,
      "principle": "SRP",
      "severity": "high",
      "description": "God class handling I/O, parsing, buffer management, number conversion, and error reporting.",
      "reasoning": "The class exceeds 4,000 lines and encapsulates multiple distinct responsibilities: stream-based I/O handling, low-level JSON token parsing, buffer management, complex numeric parsing/validation, and exception reporting. These responsibilities should be decomposed into separate components (e.g., an InputReader, a Tokenizer, and a ValueParser)."
    },
    {
      "file_path": "src/main/java/tools/jackson/core/json/UTF8JsonGenerator.java",
      "entity_name": "UTF8JsonGenerator",
      "entity_type": "class",
      "line_start": 12,
      "line_end": 2364,
      "principle": "SRP",
      "severity": "high",
      "description": "Fat class mixing business logic (generating JSON) with low-level stream/buffer management and character escaping.",
      "reasoning": "The class handles high-level JSON writing API, low-level binary output buffering, complex character encoding/escaping logic, and pretty-printing. This violates SRP as it changes for reasons related to both the JSON format definition and the underlying byte-stream output mechanisms."
    },
    {
      "file_path": "src/main/java/tools/jackson/core/JsonGenerator.java",
      "entity_name": "JsonGenerator",
      "entity_type": "class",
      "line_start": 30,
      "line_end": 2483,
      "principle": "SRP",
      "severity": "medium",
      "description": "Fat interface/abstract class forcing implementations to handle too many unrelated writing concerns.",
      "reasoning": "The class forces all implementations (JSON, binary, etc.) to implement methods for object ID writing, type ID writing, array/object manipulation, raw data writing, and POJO writing, despite many implementations only needing a subset of these capabilities (e.g., raw stream generators shouldn't necessarily implement high-level type ID logic)."
    }
  ]
}

findings.description: God class handling I/O, parsing, buffer management, number conversion, and error reporting.
findings.reasoning: The class exceeds 4,000 lines and encapsulates multiple distinct responsibilities: stream-based I/O handling, low-level JSON token parsing, buffer management, complex numeric parsing/validation, and exception reporting. These responsibilities should be decomposed into separate components (e.g., an InputReader, a Tokenizer, and a ValueParser).

findings.description: Fat class mixing business logic (generating JSON) with low-level stream/buffer management and character escaping.
findings.reasoning: The class handles high-level JSON writing API, low-level binary output buffering, complex character encoding/escaping logic, and pretty-printing. This violates SRP as it changes for reasons related to both the JSON format definition and the underlying byte-stream output mechanisms.

findings.description: Fat interface/abstract class forcing implementations to handle too many unrelated writing concerns.
findings.reasoning: The class forces all implementations (JSON, binary, etc.) to implement methods for object ID writing, type ID writing, array/object manipulation, raw data writing, and POJO writing, despite many implementations only needing a subset of these capabilities (e.g., raw stream generators shouldn't necessarily implement high-level type ID logic).
